name: Mark Warner
subject: Happy Veterans Day, Virginia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
I just wanted to send you a quick note

|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73181071/437671419/1995090618?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjA4MTcxMC1hMDgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nmwk2Yh9-gifWjQ4RashKlcBGfrGbuUl2yPSADBweT8=&emci=1e7909ab-2c7f-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=eb081710-a080-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=4568237)

| | Derek, Happy Veterans Day! Today, I hope you’ll join me in honoring all our brave veterans for the tremendous sacrifice they’ve shown in defending the ideals of American freedom and democracy at home and abroad. But this holiday serves as a reminder that veterans deserve more than just our gratitude. Those who answered the call to serve have earned the very best care. That’s why I’ve championed legislation to strengthen mental health resources and improve access to care for all of our veterans – including getting the PACT Act signed into law, which expands VA health care coverage for thousands of veterans who were exposed to toxins during their service. That’s why I’m still fighting to improve our VA facilities, provide more accessible housing for veterans and their families, and helping veterans find employment opportunities when they return home. And that’s why I’m doing everything I can to keep the extreme right faction in the House from shutting down our government – so our veterans, which make up 33% of our federal workforce and are doubly impacted by shutdowns, won’t have their key benefits suspended. Taking care of our nation’s veterans is a matter of doing what’s right. It’s as simple as that. You can always count on me to deliver for our brave veterans. Thank you and Happy Veterans Day, Mark Warner ********[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73181072/437671420/-1638483908?refcode=mw_em_231111_1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjA4MTcxMC1hMDgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nmwk2Yh9-gifWjQ4RashKlcBGfrGbuUl2yPSADBweT8=)********

Peter,
Today is Veteran’s Day. We honor the sacrifice and the courage of our veterans today, and always remember they are the reason why we’re still a free country. This morning I went on Fox and Friends to talk about our important [partnership with Team Red, White and Blue](https://mattbirk.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ce1d8ace27817bbd55d7ad20&id=b1426cc3f3&e=b2e0e42b55) \- an organization supports the mental and physical health of our veterans. I hope you’ll take the time to [watch it](https://mattbirk.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ce1d8ace27817bbd55d7ad20&id=465baea10e&e=b2e0e42b55):
[](https://mattbirk.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ce1d8ace27817bbd55d7ad20&id=c4b3eb7325&e=b2e0e42b55)
Today, you can order one of these super-premium, high quality, made-in-the-USA bags: [heritagegear.com/collections/team-rwb](https://mattbirk.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ce1d8ace27817bbd55d7ad20&id=ab47472b85&e=b2e0e42b55). Half of the proceeds will go [Team Red, White and Blue](https://mattbirk.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ce1d8ace27817bbd55d7ad20&id=f24a4a647a&e=b2e0e42b55), so they can continue serving those who proudly served our country.
Get awesome USA gear AND support the men and women that served our country- [please join us](https://mattbirk.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ce1d8ace27817bbd55d7ad20&id=7f35bcf731&e=b2e0e42b55).
And we humbly ask that God continues to bless the United States of America.

Sincerely,
Matt Birk

clean_body: Help our 3 Conservative Veteran Candidates...| | | Peter, this is Tom Cotton, Army veteran and U.S. Senator for Arkansas. This Veterans Day, I have a very important favor to ask you. I’m emailing you about three fellow veterans: in Montana, in Nevada, and - in Pennsylvania. Tim, Sam, and Dave are all running for the U.S. Senate in 2024, against tough Democrat opponents. If they don’t win, our border will stay open, inflation will stay up, crime will keep rising, and our country will become even weaker. The stakes really are that high. That’s why Tim Sheehy, Sam Brown, and Dave McCormick have once again answered the call to serve. That’s why I’m all-in to help them win. Let’s make America strong again by supporting more tough conservative veterans. In service,Tom Cotton | | Use of candidate’s military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Army, Department of the Navy, or the Department of Defense. Contributions to the National Republican Senatorial Committee are not deductible for federal income tax purposes.. | | Paid for by the NRSC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

Dear Friends and Family,

This Veterans Day, I want to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude to all the brave men and women who have served and continue to serve our great nation. Their selfless dedication and sacrifice have preserved our freedom and protected our way of life.

Let us come together as a community to honor our veterans, to remember their courage, and to recognize the challenges they face upon returning home. It is our duty to ensure they receive the support and care they deserve.

I encourage you to join me in reaching out to a veteran in your life, thanking them for their service, and considering ways we can support organizations that assist veterans in need. Together, we can make a difference in the lives of those who have given so much for us.

Happy Veterans Day to all, and may we never forget the debt of gratitude we owe to our nation's heroes.

Sincerely,

Congresswoman Kat Cammack
Florida's 3rd District

Kat Cammack is serving her second term representing the people of Northeastern Florida in Congress. She won the seat on a conservative platform of growing jobs, securing our southern border, standing up for law enforcement, defending our Second Amendment, promoting fiscal responsibility, and defending life.
Congresswoman Cammack sits on the House Committee on Energy and Commerce and House Committee on Agriculture, where she is the only Florida Republican serving the Sunshine State's farmers, ranchers, and producers. She was also appointed to the Select Subcommittee on the Weaponization of the Federal Government.
Kat and her husband Matt, who is a firefighter, paramedic, and SWAT medic, proudly call Gainesville, Florida home.

clean_body: | | | | | Dear Friends and Family, This Veterans Day, I want to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude to all the brave men and women who have served and continue to serve our great nation. Their selfless dedication and sacrifice have preserved our freedom and protected our way of life. Let us come together as a community to honor our veterans, to remember their courage, and to recognize the challenges they face upon returning home. It is our duty to ensure they receive the support and care they deserve. I encourage you to join me in reaching out to a veteran in your life, thanking them for their service, and considering ways we can support organizations that assist veterans in need. Together, we can make a difference in the lives of those who have given so much for us. Happy Veterans Day to all, and may we never forget the debt of gratitude we owe to our nation's heroes. Sincerely, Congresswoman Kat Cammack Florida's 3rd District | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | "Instagram" ---|---|--- | Kat Cammack is serving her second term representing the people of Northeastern Florida in Congress. She won the seat on a conservative platform of growing jobs, securing our southern border, standing up for law enforcement, defending our Second Amendment, promoting fiscal responsibility, and defending life. Congresswoman Cammack sits on the House Committee on Energy and Commerce and House Committee on Agriculture, where she is the only Florida Republican serving the Sunshine State's farmers, ranchers, and producers. She was also appointed to the Select Subcommittee on the Weaponization of the Federal Government. Kat and her husband Matt, who is a firefighter, paramedic, and SWAT medic, proudly call Gainesville, Florida home. | | FOLLOW KAT ONLINE | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | "Instagram" ---|---|--- Donate by Mail Kat For Congress 5200 NW 43rd Street SUITE 102-180 Gainesville, FL, 32606 | | [PAID FOR BY KAT FOR CONGRESS]() Kat Cammack 5200 NW 43rd st, suite 102-180, Gainesville, FL 32606 = \- ==

body:
Honoring our veterans and their families today and every day | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73183271/437679962/-1295403277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZDYwYTlmYS1hMjgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9j4JV1g-lhCE6At_auiB9FInlu6qghELIp3xfwr6cFw=&emci=2171b179-407f-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=3d60a9fa-a280-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=15529135) | Friends, Today, to our veterans and their families, we want to say thank you. Thank you for your courage and your sacrifice. I take my duty to give back to those who sacrificed so much very seriously. Last year, my office was honored with the distinction of a Purple Heart Congressional Office for our excellence in veterans assistance and is listed along the national Purple Heart Trail. I continue to work tirelessly to meet the needs of those who served our nation not just here in Connecticut, but through critical legislation in the House. Many veterans return home carrying visible and invisible scars, are unable to find good-paying jobs, can’t access veterans’ health care, and face high rates of homelessness. It’s unacceptable that those who served our nation lack the services they need when they come home. _That’s why I introduced the VA Mental Health Staffing Act, the Surviving Families Benefit Expansion Act, the Palomares Veterans Act, and the Honoring Our PACT Act, all of which have been signed into law by presidents of both parties. _ But our work is never over, and my commitment to our veterans and their families remains the same. I hope that you will join me today by taking a moment to remember and honor our veterans and service members and commit ourselves to serving them today and every day. – Jahana

That’s why I introduced the VA Mental Health Staffing Act, the Surviving Families Benefit Expansion Act, the Palomares Veterans Act, and the Honoring Our PACT Act, all of which have been signed into law by presidents of both parties. But our work is never over, and my commitment to our veterans and their families remains the same. I hope that you will join me today by taking a moment to remember and honor our veterans and service members and commit ourselves to serving them today and every day. – Jahana | | About Jahana** Jahana Hayes proudly represents Connecticut's 5th Congressional district. Keep up with the latest from Jahana on and . ---|--- Email is an important way for us to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. If you'd like to receive fewer emails, . If you need to update your contact information, . If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . name: Dr. Brian Williams
subject: I grew up watching my father don his Air Force uniform every day.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Brian Williams for Congress
body:
| | Peter,

Veterans Day is a deeply personal time of reflection for me. **Growing up, I proudly watched my father don his Air Force uniform each day, and felt a sense of pride knowing he was serving something greater than himself.** The legacy of military service runs deep within my family – I am the descendant of a long line of veterans dating back to the Civil War.

The twentieth-century military lineage begins with my great-grandfather, who left his Allendale farm to serve in World War I. A generation later, his son volunteered to serve in a segregated US Army unit during World War II. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73183355/437680211/1642379951?refcode=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_medium=email&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=&zip=&refcode2=aa6aa600-a380-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU5MjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWE2YWE2MDAtYTM4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6doo4vlhv3_wU1tFTGffE69qFr1IiNKK2RALycJojt4=)
_My father in his USAF uniform._ I grew up knowing that people like my father — and the generations of service members in my family — who took the oath to support and defend the Constitution did so at great sacrifice. I took this oath when I made the decision to follow in my father's footsteps of service, sacrifice, and patriotism, having earned a congressional appointment to the United States Air Force Academy.

I look back at my time in the Academy with great fondness and appreciation. We were 1,362 basic cadets preparing for a future of service for the greater good. **We answered our nation's call to continue a tradition of honor and a legacy of valor. Integrity. Excellence. Service.** | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73183357/437680220/1642379951?refcode=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_medium=email&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=&zip=&refcode2=aa6aa600-a380-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU5MjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWE2YWE2MDAtYTM4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6doo4vlhv3_wU1tFTGffE69qFr1IiNKK2RALycJojt4=) | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73183360/437680229/1642379951?refcode=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_medium=email&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=&zip=&refcode2=aa6aa600-a380-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU5MjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWE2YWE2MDAtYTM4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6doo4vlhv3_wU1tFTGffE69qFr1IiNKK2RALycJojt4=)
---|---
**Like it did to my father's life, the Air Force changed the trajectory of mine.** After four years at the Academy, I spent an additional six years on active duty, where I was promoted to 1st Lieutenant in year two and to Captain in year four. **But then, I felt a new calling to serve – this time as a doctor for my community.**

My time as a trauma surgeon didn't separate me from the world of the armed forces. In fact, it only brought to light the massive challenges our veterans face during active service and when they came home — especially in the field of healthcare. **No veteran should be without the healthcare, mental health services, or economic resources they need to thrive.**

Too often, poor policies and decisions from Washington end up harming the very people who have dedicated their lives to protecting our nation. And that's one of the main reasons I chose to run for Congress — I am answering the call to help ensure my fellow veterans have the support they deserve. **It's time for action.**

**Today, I hope you will thank a veteran in your life. If you are a veteran as well, I express my deepest gratitude for your service. Let us all stand shoulder to shoulder to ensure those who have served our country can enjoy its freedoms when they come home.**

Have a safe and meaningful Veterans Day!

Onward,

Dr. Brian Williams

_Disclaimer: Dr. Brian Williams was a commissioned officer in the United States Air Force. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the United States Air Force or the Department of Defense._ [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73183363/437680234/1642379951?refcode=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_231110_VDNA_enask_gq_act___DBW_ettx&utm_medium=email&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=&zip=&refcode2=aa6aa600-a380-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU5MjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWE2YWE2MDAtYTM4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6doo4vlhv3_wU1tFTGffE69qFr1IiNKK2RALycJojt4=)

name: Congressman Raja Krishnamoorthi
subject: Happy Veterans Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Raja for Congress
body:
Dear Peter,

Happy Veterans Day!

Today, let's all take a moment to honor those who have served in our Armed Forces and sacrificed so much for our country. They put their lives on the line to uphold our most fundamental freedoms and protect the health of our democracy.

They, along with those currently serving, are true examples of the best among us, and we owe them a debt of gratitude.

Best, safe wishes for a Happy Veterans Day.

Raja

Paid for by Friends of Raja for Congress
Raja for Congress
PO Box 681202
Schaumburg, IL 60168
United States

clean_body: there's good news too | | | SENATE MAJORITY UPDATE FOR dpwillis67@gmail.com Listen, Democrats are facing an uphill battle to save our Senate majority. We're defending a massive 23 seats — and if we lose even one of those seats, we could be handing control of the Senate to Mitch McConnell. But there's good news too: This week, Democrats flipped the Virginia House blue and held the Senate, reelected a Democratic governor in Kentucky and won a constitutional amendment to codify abortion rights in Ohio. These HUGE victories in tough, battleground states show that there's real momentum behind Democrats. But we'll only be able to replicate this next year if we invest in our Democratic incumbents NOW. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = | * | = A note from Kirsten: Friend, I can’t thank you enough for standing by my side in the critical fights ahead of us. As Democrats face our toughest Senate map in decades, I need your help more than ever. While Republicans only have to defend 10 seats in 2024, Democrats have to defend 23 — and if the GOP flips just two of them, it will deliver Senate control back to Mitch McConnell. Now, experts at FiveThirtyEight are saying that the GOP has a clear path to the majority. As right-wing special interests gear up to pour millions into flipping the Senate, we need to fight back right now. But I refuse to take a single cent from corporate PACs, which means I’m relying on your grassroots support. = And if you’d like to become a core member of my team and receive exclusive texts from my campaign, = Thank you, Kirsten Gillibrand

name: Team Kaptur
subject: What it really means to support our vets
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAPTUR FOR CONGRESS
body:
For her entire career, Marcy has made it a top priority to take care of the brave Americans who have sacrificed so much to protect our nation and our freedoms.

| |
---
Team, A lot of politicians talk about supporting our veterans, but when it comes time to actually take action, they're nowhere to be found. Some, like our opponents, stoop even lower, [misleading people on their military service](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73184379/437684091/-922216729?ms=231107_mk_UAW&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYTZjNWIxZC1hNDgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S1KHnpApgpyfDCkRHigfpFCsjbc1hzSuzLncOuuLl6g=&emci=fbbb61f5-4a7f-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=da6c5b1d-a480-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=2814549) and cheering for a government shutdown that would deny veterans their pay. **Luckily, we have leaders like Marcy, who show us what it really means to support our vets.** For her entire career, Marcy has made it a top priority to take care of the brave Americans who have sacrificed so much to protect our nation and our freedoms. She's spearheaded major federal investments in Ohio's defense bases, and been a steadfast advocate for veterans' healthcare and benefits. As long as Marcy is in Congress, veterans will always have a strong voice. Thanks for being a part of our team, friend, and thank you to all who have served.

— Team Kaptur

name: Matt Cartwright
subject: Veterans Day 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cartwright for Congress
body:
On this Veterans Day, let us pay tribute to the brave men and women who have defended our nation.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73184467/437684417/1790176412?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzgyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOTZjNWIxZC1hNDgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aZu4iCPZqHMkhCiOKHGBuNTOKo9adReBSF2jbWAFREI=&emci=8a69c8ad-3b7f-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=d96c5b1d-a480-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=23311593) | Peter,

Today, we pause to reflect and honor the courage, dedication, and sacrifices of the men and women who have served our nation in the Armed Forces. Veterans Day is a time to recognize their extraordinary commitment and to express our gratitude for their selfless service.

Veterans Day reminds us that the freedom and way of life we enjoy come at a cost, one that our veterans and their families have willingly borne. Through times of peace and times of conflict, they’ve stood up to protect our nation, safeguard our liberties, and preserve our values.

**We owe our veterans a debt that can never truly be repaid.**

This day is an occasion to thank them not just through words, but through our actions and unwavering support. Our veterans deserve access to high-quality healthcare, educational opportunities, meaningful employment, and the social services necessary to ensure their well-being – it is our responsibility to create a supportive environment where our veterans can thrive in their return to civilian life.

On this Veterans Day, let us pay tribute to the brave men and women who have defended our nation. Let us remember that the freedoms we enjoy and the democracy we cherish are the result of their dedication.

To our veterans: Thank you for your service, your sacrifices, and your unwavering devotion to our country. Your heroism is an inspiration to us all, and your contributions will forever hold a special place in the heart of our nation.

Sincerely,

Matt
---

This Veterans Day, I want to extend my deepest appreciation and respect to our brave veterans who have sacrificed so much for our country. Their unwavering commitment to defending our freedoms and values is an inspiration to us all.

Let us take this opportunity to reflect on the sacrifices made by our servicemen and women and express our gratitude for their service. We must also remember that supporting veterans extends beyond one day – it is a lifelong commitment. I am dedicated to working tirelessly on policies and initiatives that ensure our veterans receive the care, support, and opportunities they deserve.

Thank you for your continued support, and let us all come together to honor and salute our veterans on this important day.

-Congressman Lawler

clean_body: | | | | We thank you. | This Veterans Day, I want to extend my deepest appreciation and respect to our brave veterans who have sacrificed so much for our country. Their unwavering commitment to defending our freedoms and values is an inspiration to us all. Let us take this opportunity to reflect on the sacrifices made by our servicemen and women and express our gratitude for their service. We must also remember that supporting veterans extends beyond one day – it is a lifelong commitment. I am dedicated to working tirelessly on policies and initiatives that ensure our veterans receive the care, support, and opportunities they deserve. Thank you for your continued support, and let us all come together to honor and salute our veterans on this important day. -Congressman Lawler | | | | | Mike Lawler is a Republican running for reelection in New York’s 17th Congressional District. He is passionate about standing with law enforcement and making our communities more safe, protecting Social Security and Medicare, fixing our crumbling roads and bridges, and restoring the SALT deduction to lower taxes and make New York more affordable. | | USE THE ADDRESS BELOW TO CONTRIBUTE BY CHECK LAWLER FOR CONGRESS PO BOX 87 SOUTH SALEM, NY 10590 GET YOUR MIKE LAWLER, NO CUOMO MERCH No New Yorker wants to call our Tappan Zee Bridge the “Governor Mario M. Cuomo Bridge”. Proudly send the message that you want the bridge restored as the Tappan Zee. | Are you interested in staying up-to-date on Mike Lawler's reelection battle? Don't miss out on the latest news and updates from his campaign! By texting "Lawler" to 85007, you can receive regular updates on Mike's campaign, upcoming events, and important news about the race. name: Speaker Mike Johnson
subject: Are you sure this is correct?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE JOHNSON FOR LOUISIANA
body:
It’s been a crazy couple of weeks, so I understand if you’re not on Team Johnson yet. We’re emailing today to make sure we have the information we need to contact you. | [Unsubscribe](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_s=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_t=4eG3gDqDGaUJcEopP879XiRkRogLH4Vc1TCDEvWAzo_e6M-1xjOE-qyf4nRbel8iKe9MA47ChiN-_lPoAu3Jq8OJ4IyhZOWwKy_6zA4GGIU%3D) | [Mark as Junk](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_s=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_t=4eG3gDqDGaUJcEopP879XiRkRogLH4Vc1TCDEvWAzo_e6M-1xjOE-qyf4nRbel8iKe9MA47ChiN-_lPoAu3Jq8OJ4IyhZOWwKy_6zA4GGIU%3D)
[ ](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWaZ8NnNvYnL29rRhDxUGArPXIVpdMpZSdgHGWsDcAfwoCfJS6m1zBk7OYSmaygxuh-EjHR4FiQe_Ugt7mJ5TsYPRmkpHX4mYYYIob7nFkdB6m8Vj7OJek7HHs4q996Ar5hWu3VnFjcK8woOM3lhSOvrm7gmPzPAZMbm_UnGTaWNRA%3D)
---
Peter -

I’m still settling into my new office – both the physical space and the position I now hold in the federal government. I’m going to be honest with you, the last few weeks have been a blur – but productive and exciting as well.

Believe it or not, there wasn’t a manual left behind that I can reference in this new role. So, as you can imagine, we’ve been wasting no time settling in and figuring out how to best prioritize your interests and needs. The American people deserve nothing less than my best and that’s what I intend to give.

[**I’m reaching out today because I want to make sure that my team has the appropriate email, phone number, and mailing address on file for you.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWapqDkY0E2vEPEYkfqWLpX6mQL0qRb-riwrzq39wg8zgGijoZPnzksa-TZ9JJGr7gVTK4XuoJpfl2rBlyj2BV9RNGNijz3eRERfK_KJ171-GmuGKx1yXHpP6GReSTPjGJAMjUxghqh8OqgXQCutxHJkq1OtV9S7AzFxCowQ3cE6ak%3D)
[** UPDATE OUR RECORDS **](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWaowVGWveBJ9eF6jG3uzO3P6V0czHdV67n8OXv_ZTlBlCq5f20eF6F430LCMeZJqUg-lHMSI5N7fzlq1uPASzCYIgC25L-nXfeigGBhbyk1sxCMJY_4WrUdl255j1W9qA5E4XVMZC207s0KcgYl74YcD2wdbJJY1kHpcWJcdsJ2SM%3D)
Before my colleagues elected me the 56th Speaker of the House, our mailing list was mainly comprised of folks from the great state of Louisiana. _But now as Speaker, every Republican in the country is a constituent of mine and I need to be able to know I can reach out as needed._

Holding on to the House majority in next November’s election is going to be a tall order – [**but with you on our team, I know our chances will increase exponentially.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWa2rMyJo-Ea_UiTIL5vgf5nKXGJ9zHN-XViwPxDed3KA_NaE2s9g0gY4dLpXeL7-Yu76TmiG10ZfuVD61GnYWGKZn-JjIu6fTK9ciQxelMKzPHkKFY3sRs1YnW8FXNGJbud3KkTLTQty8T4pFFbiRDHbbfqDZ5xupofzQlPbC04kQ%3D)

I’ll be honest with you, before I was elected to lead the House a few weeks ago, I was relatively unknown nationally, meaning I probably didn’t have you on my mailing list. As we build out our campaign operation from the ground up, _**I am going to need every patriot to step forward.**_ So please, [**let me hear from you today.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWajCXkNWpY4AmmMQbkCGyPuRM7LSQEz6NIghjHLWoRbyo2jijkzcDVF_ALpbgZ-FcvApNjLjfcYJQwTZbIRxGIZ7S0s9Nwdn9Ltk5--Z6Dnr_YlGHqWLO_No0ZvMpsb0FsOfd4-H90khKs3Mum4naJqlRqAc8dCTgd37Olm__HPB8%3D)

[**There are many more conservatives I haven’t reached yet.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWasK3DzO25BtQqOqaxMOgpzi95dKO9AmElSLYBBSNPmxuvzR4djU4aBVenEIlUKUrxP4WK6ggpfEZu4oAhKauoyyKXRlkb7n2H15GUliJ57jNJeJNAId6RngU6TM27FBgGxogzKkPNePYLZzUmTX_VV0vVv0hzQk3WpJ2VLWmvn0o%3D)

[** _RESPOND TO THE SPEAKER HERE_ **](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWacWKq8Ysyk4xsPrpbP78AZp7ZOYh3M_a-6gGSnSnJ9bOTCLaYuOdEGjZWpv_M3twTlBiyT5TvAokBUqEKT0sFFZBop9XQzl0PgNqWpIiB3KezOS0jnKnjCVRAsSRtkhFmm9IwaB6_KoiaDAKNwGijpiOLPfbOWmVs4gv48iMZMAA%3D)
With respect,

[ ](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=72720b268d2a451493166f74758cca4b&_e=BPTMWsoWgShXbY0p8ueXsafrD1Zf8qJVD8YRzvQHUONa3Ayl25tPQ-klYl7sVqysmzIayome1znOhU6cxQpJzTtFAKelafUM-QoLS3uPhIGJgDverYJTs9mqJrtSaslMC0dz_Cw1EEyyN0C0KuDu43PBeLpoA8Bk_aLJ2oay9HisdEgdyzb3oROA492dHsVmfTbnnGZQg-x_g6_YqgMIXw93563qtcOY4zgqYHY_iJfG6MujBtaMeDf-8VRBOrWaFEG4ZXRfPRlY4cFoQsbFXLtfcz1OSszJ_huk7rpyscLFG-tud-Gv6VxLNM8Va6e69uyVIXr7xMgUShSNKrcCtSANodt9GcHjZonYqhSsHdPa-e4OVVJai9wv1qmh50KLmRnm3PaLmJvHPvtGlxcRPqQPH5PzgL1G_q3z6rc5pZs%3D)

Mike Johnson
Speaker of the House

* * *

As Speaker, I want to hear from you on the top issues of the day. It's important for us to deliver results for the American people.

clean_body: It’s been a crazy couple of weeks, so I understand if you’re not on Team Johnson yet. We’re emailing today to make sure we have the information we need to contact you. | | Peter - I’m still settling into my new office – both the physical space and the position I now hold in the federal government. I’m going to be honest with you, the last few weeks have been a blur – but productive and exciting as well. Believe it or not, there wasn’t a manual left behind that I can reference in this new role. So, as you can imagine, we’ve been wasting no time settling in and figuring out how to best prioritize your interests and needs. The American people deserve nothing less than my best and that’s what I intend to give. Before my colleagues elected me the 56th Speaker of the House, our mailing list was mainly comprised of folks from the great state of Louisiana. But now as Speaker, every Republican in the country is a constituent of mine and I need to be able to know I can reach out as needed. Holding on to the House majority in next November’s election is going to be a tall order – I’ll be honest with you, before I was elected to lead the House a few weeks ago, I was relatively unknown nationally, meaning I probably didn’t have you on my mailing list. As we build out our campaign operation from the ground up, I am going to need every patriot to step forward. So please, With respect, Mike Johnson Speaker of the House As Speaker, I want to hear from you on the top issues of the day. It's important for us to deliver results for the American people. Would you take a moment to complete my today? Your input is important to making sure we move America forward. I appreciate you taking the time to send me your input. | SPEAKER MIKE JOHNSON Speaker Johnson is a dedicated husband and father of four, as well as a constitutional law attorney who has devoted his life and career to fighting for the fundamental freedoms and traditional values that have always been a priority to the people of Louisiana. | PAID FOR BY MIKE JOHNSON FOR LOUISIANA Don't want to receive any more of our emails? body:
This is time-sensitive.
| | | Hey there -- It’s Diana, the Research Director here at the DSCC. I’ve got some BIG news: **We’ve selected YOU to represent Pennsylvania in a Democratic Strategy Interview for today, 11/11/23.**

Only a select few of our most committed grassroots Democrats were chosen for this _incredible_ opportunity, so you have a _rare_ chance to help shape our vital 2024 Senate strategy and defeat Republicans next year!

Today’s survey closes at 11:59 PM, and **we won’t have a complete dataset without your response! So please, click this personal link to enter your Democratic Strategy Interview by taking the survey now:
[www.dscc.org/DailyStrategyInterview#9716520](http://go.dscc.org/go/15921?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20231111-Diana-DianaDailyStrategy-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20231111-Diana-DianaDailyStrategy-Pros-sl1&akid=12546%2E9716520%2EyDkUMw)**

| **[START YOUR INTERVIEW](http://go.dscc.org/go/15921?ask=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20231111-Diana-DianaDailyStrategy-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20231111-Diana-DianaDailyStrategy-Pros-sl1&akid=12546%2E9716520%2EyDkUMw)**

name: Gwen S. Moore
subject: Honoring our veterans today, and every day
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Gwen Moore for Congress
body:
My Sisters and Brothers,

Today holds immense significance for America. It's Veterans Day – a moment dedicated solely to expressing gratitude to the brave men and women who selflessly served in America's military, safeguarding our cherished way of life. These remarkable individuals personify the very essence of our nation – embodying heroism by placing the interests of our country above their own.

As a nation, it is our duty to reciprocate their service in any way we can. While we can never fully repay our debt to them, we must do all that we can to enhance their healthcare, expand their benefits, and ensure they are presented with every opportunity once they transition from their esteemed service.

Let us set aside political differences today and focus on demonstrating our heartfelt appreciation.

If you haven't done so already, take a moment to express your gratitude to a veteran.

Together, #WeRepresent!

Gwen
Proudly representing Wisconsin's 4th Congressional District
Proud Co-Leader of the Violence Against Women Act
100% Pro-Choice
100% Democracy Defender

About Gwen: The first African-American woman in Wisconsin history to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives, Congresswoman Gwen Moore, took her official oath of office on January 4, 2005. She served in the Wisconsin State Senate from 1993-2004. Before her election to the Senate, Congresswoman Moore served two consecutive terms in the Wisconsin State Assembly from 1989-92.

name: Glenn Grothman
subject: Honoring America's Veterans
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=f9ef163c38f94024905cc6b5e55f222e&_m=9f2cfe5382a2418abada149f20a65094&_e=q9pnN5S92V1Bn24LUSndWtrV2AhRer_tEhMe6dHqlu4w9d6OiB1inBXU0hOlPEwVtWtMd34mpU4waYIK5ybCIcRDY13puDt4gh_MrcNpo2-9RAZu9fdOxOunTdMHSC-8s7cbCLwIg_XRFqCZqO3HdlPxiz-YoiiCPzih7MVCHgChyM7tWkdsvS37gwQKvzGeu4Q5kxcLoSfKMYdwOMl0uw%3D%3D)
---
My friend, This Veteran’s Day, I am honored to thank all of those who have served our nation in the Armed Services. As your member of Congress, I have had the privilege of meeting with many of our nation’s bravest who have fought for our country’s liberties. These men and women have sacrificed comfort, time with families, and even their own lives to protect all of us. I am moved beyond words by their service. To all of you who have served our country, thank you! Your service to our country is an inspiration to us all. As your representative, I will always work to make sure you receive the respect and support you have earned and deserve. Happy Veteran’s Day! Sincerely, Glenn
---

name: Tim Sheehy, Navy SEAL
subject: Something I never felt before
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA
body:
When I saw everything that we’d worked for in Afghanistan washed away in a matter of days, I decided to consider serving in government once again | [Unsubscribe](http://links.g4yamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_s=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_t=vbAQyilACozEjEJ3mUmq_2DIy0v8VvBoOqxPJvaBB_FKEoHoOyvG_iNBi6DaNRfnmRPVNxDXy_pcczYqflBnKPb5i9HnJmEylcRaGPjwsis%3D) | [Mark as Junk](http://links.g4yamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_s=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_t=vbAQyilACozEjEJ3mUmq_2DIy0v8VvBoOqxPJvaBB_FKEoHoOyvG_iNBi6DaNRfnmRPVNxDXy_pcczYqflBnKPb5i9HnJmEylcRaGPjwsis%3D)
---
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsITSdiQreC_Y1T-aorHJlM7UAAUwVX3KfeH-Up2fVxw876IoCRqK6KzbHM9jnKNzSHx4c2VqxAyAYAhkNWdnEi4LBmgZMVxJYM0bfH0APRo7R5XbvW68iqvq1r6Z4ZqjB1JbJTb7h7nYv3XAOBjZ7bk%3D)

Fellow Conservative,

As a former Navy SEAL and combat veteran, I’ve seen my fair share of battle. Between my and my wife’s service to our country, we’ve sacrificed years of our lives to make America a more safe and prosperous nation and to protect our God-given freedoms and liberties. We served so that our children and yours might grow up to realize their own American Dreams.

Duty to country. Service and sacrifice over self, **those are the traits that define me**. I’m proud of the work we did for our country. That said, the events of August 2021 rocked me to my core and ultimately led me to **[launch a campaign for the United States Senate this past June.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsAPs1kpDqWCineJnHYpOBjOcxiU5u_RfVkKswnEevheQp2P352mryQG4V-Xz7T6l_PH89-GjVhDR-6JrkVwXmRK1p1TMg_lvxBKDK2evzGM-GJJ9N47TdgaIXPPrRHjJUMp-2-YxV2XgV2zFUMvOUf4%3D)**

In August 2021, I was putting out wildfires in California by day as part of the aerial firefighting company that I run; by night, my colleagues [many veterans like me] and I were working the phones trying to get our friends and allies out of Afghanistan before the takeover by the Taliban. I remember vividly that at one point I was trying to help an ally **sneak through a tunnel to get him and his family to the airport** so that they could be evacuated on a U.S. military aircraft. The Taliban wanted these high-value targets dead; they wanted their families dead.

[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsIT3l26Y9jsbkK6cgrhr2hTwWOQ8hx0syDp2h5H6-L2Kjx1nvpEJLtUArZSAkYIk9f-GfU-bca31wUj-rVtJAAvqoPoTdGzw4XKvMjlXR--zSHiwSB9HHlWIXRPvxeypOqDPy2YnJ51OJSrlerq5n_A%3D)
This continued for days. Throughout the entire process, _**“I kept thinking to myself, “The adult will come in the room and say, ‘Hey, this is a disaster; we’re going to stop this.’”**_

Every day it got worse.
Every day it was a disaster.
The adult in the room never materialized.

**Then, 13 Americans were vaporized at the gate to Kabul’s airport.** Billions of dollars in military and human assets were left behind – all so Joe Biden could get a press conference saying that he’d pulled the country out of an “endless war.”

I sat in the homes of the families that we were frantically trying to get out; I promised them that we weren’t going to leave them behind. _**So, you can imagine the dishonor that I felt**_ because of the promises we weren’t able to keep to the people who’d sacrificed so much to help us combat evil.

It was in the aftermath of the fall of Afghanistan that I ever truly considered entering politics. Deciding to launch a campaign for the United States Senate wasn’t an easy decision. **But the United States needs leaders who understand the sacrifice it’s taken to build our country. When your country calls and needs you, you have to answer. **

[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsEqKfYthxWnf_9uKP8cECKypY86UigwJ0lG3iGgpwEs3IY5Y0XFHyg_ICQhWBqEqoVxNhpSNA0ApMGdSHc1zDfpVM-NfHCdNvwcUF_a48ovteCniaJ7BqUzIEwiPR_n7YnkfGbq5bgQ_HL8y4EQtVB0%3D)
**Unlike Joe Biden, I understand what it is to serve our country. Not service in politics, but on the battlefield.** I’ve been wounded in service of our nation; I’ve lost mentors and friends who were willing to make the ultimate sacrifice so that future generations will be free. [**MAKE A GIFT TODAY
HELP ME WIN THIS RACE->** ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsFFQaD0hoDzNVkWvu22Y0iusiw48cUngCZFXK9eSK8hPNDQItbIe36d1Pccp4sGferUN_Kr86EbosNNdjii6lTKDkhXD7RYDyrEz5Za7qG9ZH1T6Yvyzcg9xQ8OV5gqgu3d5uD3EE0T0IofSTxom_9I%3D)

I’m reaching out to you today because if this campaign to flip Montana’s U.S. Senate seat is going to be successful, **[I’m going to need the help of every conservative donor who reads this letter.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsBKdGGyDrPIzV5nQugF2klGA-BoAiQ6sI3-7M-iPRuMgspuzc-6thpNKg6odz55MSRILonK4aAFccz3PCYrNY6IuxTE3UMq3xNJrbQmGNi1FRerRQyFg_JO8uVceQTpVcii7NBHwgtofzcEwPIxrnt8%3D) **

Twice in a row, Joe Biden has pulled us out of two wars for political purposes.

We fought in Iraq, _**I was there.**_ We fought in Afghanistan, _**I was there.**_

And each time, for political purposes, Biden pulled out our forces and damaged our nation’s reputation in the process. And my opponent in the Senate race, Democrat Jon Tester, supported Biden both times.

I want to look at my children and tell them, _**“Yes, you should serve your country, as your mom and dad did. Your parents put on the uniform; you should do the same.”**_

[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsKtIWrIe1lyPtC0s3mXuLsXctPNUGO3eezRgh5lWMZUDf7DsuI15nZKx2d6qchR9ubNoxHRNtigqqUKDqpMONivKOi4e_farfdlqzrBLbD4xlcEfeUGtapStn5BvwriC2GAJF1VN08_8SBaQZYMe3gg%3D)
**But when we have a president who is willing to pull the plug on a whim for political purposes, it’s hard to tell future generations the sacrifices in service of God and country will be worth it.**

So, Fellow Conservative \- that’s what I’m fighting for. I’m fighting to restore faith in our American way of life. I entered the political arena to ensure the sacrifices my friends made weren’t made in vain.

[**HELP ME FLIP THIS SEAT
ELECT A FIGHTER TO THE U.S. SENATE->** ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c24e90ffaae34d3a89e34823dfecfc3d&_e=Mjd7XZUcXR8hfSA-S_OY_fLB_L0EaA7CsA9XYF9UvMlYLW6eF7J3j1y8Td5ltQ47Z3ktWnGicPM3S93bTHILqE5924ZwGOPlRDZ_xMn7Z8IO3iBadEiS2-AHHcH9WxMqEr0hhGatq1AlGtb3rkGLpElMrIGSMYZpc57e5m59OiPKw-ABD544Tnu0WqzT-BSgiX6tvyDnWg-DfTIflxJrfBo7-Xn-CeTQliLrrJ-rFEuSDdBnKJTWZqX6RijghYSW5GcerGdvkwQYSKwLecxKsJBKvvt4ugipk9Zhan8Mbq81G6IgbHdWBxjPX4Xuu8J584ICCkKzQVf5vD5_-rGFuni9Co89TsFufotb3SoZoznNu1mUikmhIK2OFv4CripY5S_J1FPA-Sf2d7GAL_bGTcFCiUruTdAQ3StSnFedWaw%3D)

Peter,This Veterans Day, I am reminded of the incredible sacrifices made by our brave men and women in uniform, both past and present. It is a day to express our gratitude for their unwavering dedication to preserving our freedom and protecting our great nation.

Texas has a long and storied tradition of military service, and our veterans have played a crucial role in shaping our state's history. They continue to inspire us with their commitment to duty, honor, and country.

Let us remember that veterans are not just heroes on November 11th, but every day. They are our neighbors, our friends, and our family members. It is our duty to stand by them, just as they have stood by us.

Thank you to all the veterans for your service and sacrifice. Your dedication to our nation does not go unnoticed, and I am proud to represent you in Congress.

Happy Veterans Day!

Roger Williams is a Christian, conservative, pro-life, and second amendment supporter taking Texas values to Washington, D.C. He is a small business owner with over 45 years of experience and is running for Congress to create economic growth, jobs, and provide strong leadership. Roger worked alongside President Trump to fight against high taxes, rising energy prices, and soaring healthcare costs. His passion for conservative leadership led him to support Republican candidates from the White House to the county courthouse. Roger serves on the Board of Trustees for the Bush School of Government and Public Service at Texas A&M University, the Texas Christian University Board of Trustees, and the National Football Foundation and College Football Hall of Fame Board of Directors.

clean_body: | | | | Peter,This Veterans Day, I am reminded of the incredible sacrifices made by our brave men and women in uniform, both past and present. It is a day to express our gratitude for their unwavering dedication to preserving our freedom and protecting our great nation. Texas has a long and storied tradition of military service, and our veterans have played a crucial role in shaping our state's history. They continue to inspire us with their commitment to duty, honor, and country. Let us remember that veterans are not just heroes on November 11th, but every day. They are our neighbors, our friends, and our family members. It is our duty to stand by them, just as they have stood by us. Thank you to all the veterans for your service and sacrifice. Your dedication to our nation does not go unnoticed, and I am proud to represent you in Congress. Happy Veterans Day! Rep. Roger Williams TX-25 | Roger Williams is a Christian, conservative, pro-life, and second amendment supporter taking Texas values to Washington, D.C. He is a small business owner with over 45 years of experience and is running for Congress to create economic growth, jobs, and provide strong leadership. Roger worked alongside President Trump to fight against high taxes, rising energy prices, and soaring healthcare costs. His passion for conservative leadership led him to support Republican candidates from the White House to the county courthouse. Roger serves on the Board of Trustees for the Bush School of Government and Public Service at Texas A&M University, the Texas Christian University Board of Trustees, and the National Football Foundation and College Football Hall of Fame Board of Directors. | "Facebook" | "Twitter" ---|--- | | ⬇DONATE BY CHECK⬇ ROGER WILLIAMS FOR U.S. CONGRESS 10 N. CADDO ST. PMB #174 CLEBURNE, TX 76031 Stay connected with our campaign and be part of the movement. Follow Roger on social media for updates, volunteer to support the cause, or reach out to him directly. By joining Team Williams, you will make a difference in your community. | A Note For You, From Roger My commitment to our great state and country is unwavering, and I am grateful to have my wife Patty, our two daughters, and YOU standing beside me on this journey. | Williams For Congress 10 N. name: Gloria Johnson
subject: Thank you, veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gloria for Tennessee
body:
Let’s remember the people behind the holiday.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73189788/437704525/-849459103?refcode=ea.a8f1a012-ac80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ms=ea.a8f1a012-ac80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGYxYTAxMi1hYzgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=w5bU5GwoDgZywNwUk03AajVLyW1bI5w5XIcaAyCrIwY=&emci=93351dda-3b7f-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=a8f1a012-ac80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=46764483)
---
Hi there, As we celebrate Veterans Day, it’s so important that we all remember the _people_ behind the holiday. Our veterans put their bodies on the line to defend this country and our freedoms. They heard the call and stepped up. Their families sacrificed, too — missing out on time with their loved ones while steadfastly supporting their commitment to service. America has a long history of service, all the way back to our founding. We would not be the nation we are today without courageous men and women devoting themselves to the greater good. We can’t repay our debt to our veterans, but they do deserve to have their commitment honored. Politicians and policymakers have an obligation to advocate for veterans’ interests. As your senator, I’ll fight to ensure they have access to the physical and mental health care and support that they need; support policies that help veterans reintegrate when their service ends; and seek out their input on other issues affecting veterans. It’s not enough, but it’s the least our veterans deserve. Yours in service, Gloria Johnson [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73189789/437704526/-849459103?refcode=ea.a8f1a012-ac80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ms=ea.a8f1a012-ac80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGYxYTAxMi1hYzgwLWVlMTEtODkyNS0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=w5bU5GwoDgZywNwUk03AajVLyW1bI5w5XIcaAyCrIwY=&emci=93351dda-3b7f-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=a8f1a012-ac80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=46764483)

clean_body: Let’s remember the people behind the holiday. | Hi there, As we celebrate Veterans Day, it’s so important that we all remember the people behind the holiday. Our veterans put their bodies on the line to defend this country and our freedoms. They heard the call and stepped up. Their families sacrificed, too — missing out on time with their loved ones while steadfastly supporting their commitment to service. America has a long history of service, all the way back to our founding. We would not be the nation we are today without courageous men and women devoting themselves to the greater good. We can’t repay our debt to our veterans, but they do deserve to have their commitment honored. Politicians and policymakers have an obligation to advocate for veterans’ interests. As your senator, I’ll fight to ensure they have access to the physical and mental health care and support that they need; support policies that help veterans reintegrate when their service ends; and seek out their input on other issues affecting veterans. It’s not enough, but it’s the least our veterans deserve. Yours in service, Gloria Johnson | Follow along: | | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Gloria for Tennessee Gloria for Tennessee 73 White Bridge Rd #103-353 Nashville, TN 37205

name: Michael B. Moore
subject: On this Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael B
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73190642/437707192/-1369586090?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.11.11_B1_VeteransDay_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ1ODUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjdhYmY4NzQtYWQ4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aDvUCYs8mOCChzhUpfXTmuTZsrsflF48lTkEROcQWXU=&emci=fe642940-7e7e-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=b7abf874-ad80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=14781535) | Peter – Today, we think of the brave men and women who have served our country in the armed forces. It's their heroism that has allowed us to exercise our rights and freedoms at home. Their sacrifices and selflessness deserve our highest commendation. I'm also thinking of my great-great-grandfather, Robert Smalls, who served in the Navy. Born into slavery, he later became a pilot on a Confederate ship – _Planter_ – and steered enslaved people to freedom, disguising himself as a Confederate captain. After traveling north, he surrendered the ship to the Union Navy. This act would not only earn his freedom, but other Black soldiers and their families aboard the _Planter_. He later served our country as a Congressman here in South Carolina. We are forever in debt for the service of our Veterans, and I couldn't be prouder that my family shares in the tradition of service to our country. It's important that we honor them with the benefits they deserve – from quality healthcare to affordable housing. It is the least we can do to show our appreciation. Wishing you a reflective Veterans Day, Michael B. Moore [MEET MICHAEL](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73190643/437707193/1446694319?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.11.11_B1_VeteransDay_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ1ODUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjdhYmY4NzQtYWQ4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aDvUCYs8mOCChzhUpfXTmuTZsrsflF48lTkEROcQWXU=&emci=fe642940-7e7e-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=b7abf874-ad80-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=14781535) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73190644/437707195/983305604?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.11.11_B1_VeteransDay_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ1ODUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjdhYmY4NzQtYWQ4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aDvUCYs8mOCChzhUpfXTmuTZsrsflF48lTkEROcQWXU=)

clean_body: | Peter – Today, we think of the brave men and women who have served our country in the armed forces. It's their heroism that has allowed us to exercise our rights and freedoms at home. Their sacrifices and selflessness deserve our highest commendation. I'm also thinking of my great-great-grandfather, Robert Smalls, who served in the Navy. Born into slavery, he later became a pilot on a Confederate ship – Planter – and steered enslaved people to freedom, disguising himself as a Confederate captain. After traveling north, he surrendered the ship to the Union Navy. This act would not only earn his freedom, but other Black soldiers and their families aboard the Planter. He later served our country as a Congressman here in South Carolina. We are forever in debt for the service of our Veterans, and I couldn't be prouder that my family shares in the tradition of service to our country. It's important that we honor them with the benefits they deserve – from quality healthcare to affordable housing. It is the least we can do to show our appreciation. Wishing you a reflective Veterans Day, Michael B. Moore = | | Paid for by Michael B. Moore for South Carolina Michael B. **I have always known the importance of a good team.** _It’s why I married such a phenomenal woman and surround myself with God-fearing, hard-working, driven individuals every single day._

It's important for us to deliver results for the American people. Would you take a moment to complete my today? Your input is important to making sure we move America forward. I appreciate you taking the time to send me your input. | SPEAKER MIKE JOHNSON Speaker Johnson is a dedicated husband and father of four, as well as a constitutional law attorney who has devoted his life and career to fighting for the fundamental freedoms and traditional values that have always been a priority to the people of Louisiana. | PAID FOR BY MIKE JOHNSON FOR LOUISIANA Don't want to receive any more of our emails? name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I’m thinking of my three big brothers today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
We have to back our platitudes with meaningful action and policies that support our veterans

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=1814%2E4032634%2EK0ElnO)
---
Every Veterans Day, I think of my three big brothers — Don Reed, John, and David — who all served in the military. And I think of all the families who have sacrificed so much. |
---

After they went away, my mother would wait by the front door six days a week for the mail. The question of the day was always “Is there something from one of the boys?”
When a letter came from one of the boys, the day was extra-sunny. She would read it and re-read it and call my aunts or my grandmother to read it again over the phone. My daddy would get home from work and she would be waiting with the letter in her hand.
And on the days where a letter didn’t come, she would say, “Well, maybe tomorrow. Maybe tomorrow.”
She cherished those letters. We all did.
My brothers all made it home safely, and for that, I’m eternally grateful.
Growing up with brothers in the military gave Veterans Day a special meaning. I understand the responsibility we have to our veterans, and the unique burdens that military families carry.
In Washington, I’ll keep pushing our government to make decisions in the best interest of our service members, their families, and our national security. To take care of our veterans when they come home.
We have to back our platitudes with meaningful action and policies that support our military both during and after service — good health care, quality and affordable housing, opportunities for a good education and a job.
No matter how you honor Veterans Day, I hope you have a meaningful day.
Elizabeth

---
| | | |
---

Sincerely,

Jake LaTurner

name: Wesley Bell
subject: Celebrating the 355,000 veterans that call Missouri home
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
|
---

Happy Veterans Day, Peter.

Today, we take the time to honor the bravery and selflessness of the many service members who risked their lives to defend our nation.

More than 355,000 veterans live in Missouri, and nearly 34,000 of them call Missouri's 1st district home. These veterans live amongst us as our friends, family, and neighbors who have made incredible sacrifices to keep us safe and protect our democracy, and for that, we'll be forever indebted to them.

This Veterans Day, we remember those who put everything on the line to serve our country. Because the challenges veterans face don't end when their service does, we must commit to honoring and supporting our veterans as they continue to return home and their families.

My message to all veterans in Missouri and across the country is this: **Thank you for your service, sacrifice, and commitment to protecting our country.**

Thank you,

Wesley Bell

PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI

7253 WATSON RD #1115, ST LOUIS, MO 63119

name: Josh McConkey
subject: Army Combat Veteran, Air Force Commander, and Emergency Doctor- McConkey for Congress- Campaign Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McConkey for Congress
body:
Friends and Neighbors,

I wanted to reach out and update each of you now that the campaign is in full swing. We were waiting for those district lines, which were finally released in November. With the holidays fast approaching, I want to wish you a blessed holiday season. Thank you for your support in my bid to bring foundational leadership and change to our country. It's a huge task, and I need each of you to spread the good word. My book, Be the Weight Behind the Spear, is my call to action for North Carolinians, and we'll find out on March 5th, 2024, if they care or not. I know they do!

Please help contribute if you're able. Even $20 helps, as small donations help pad those stats for numbers of donors!

Our congressional lines are finally approved, and I am perfectly positioned to unseat freshman Congressman Wiley Nickel who votes with Joe Biden 94% of the time. I think you'll like my campaign ad on border security. Please let me know when you have time for a phone call. We have an opportunity to gain 4 additional Republican seats in the House with your help!

Please check out my campaign ad:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3eON8r0CU5o

As an emergency physician and current Air Force Reserve commander (and Army combat veteran), I know to cut through the chaos, you must keep it simple! That’s why my campaign is about the 3 S’s:

• Security

• Safety

• Service

I'm running a Congressional race that is the 5th most Republican leaning seat in the country that we don't currently hold. As a special forces medical director, I place a premium on time, and I value your time immensely. If you have 5 minutes to talk, please text or call me at the number below.

North Carolina families deserve:

• Economic and Border Security!

• Physical safety for their children!

• Selfless service from their elected leaders!

I need your support to help me take our country back. I have a direct shot at unseating freshman Congressman Wiley Nickel (votes with Biden 94% of the time), who has tons of liberal California money flowing in to prop him up. I raised nearly $365,000 out of the gates, but I need to keep the momentum going. I have a critical deadline for the 4th quarter coming up at Midnight EST on December 31st and it would mean a lot to this Army and Air Force veteran if you could contribute and help me for this all-important filing deadline.

My primary issues are border security and the fentanyl crisis that is killing over 122,000 Americans per year. As an emergency doctor, I deal with this daily and we must do more to protect our children! I know you understand the importance of having healthcare experience in Congress- please check out my website and my "Pathway to Victory" (attached).

Josh McConkey for Congress<https://www.joshmcconkey.com/>

"Be the weight behind the spear", this is the ethos I use to lead myself and my team to emphasize the importance of hard work, dedication, and a strong sense of purpose in achieving success. I am an Air Force Reserve Colonel, emergency room physician, prior professor (Duke Univ), community servant, and most importantly, a husband to a beautiful wife and proud father to three great children.

Unlike Wiley, I’m here to solve problems that are challenging Americans and their families. Things like supporting our veterans and fixing the VA, solving the opioid crises facing our Nation, and getting our economy back on track. We must address the bloated government spending and our national debt approaching $33 trillion!

My race is critical to holding and expanding the GOP Majority in the House, and I hope we can count on your support. Respectfully, I hope you will visit my website, www.joshmcconkey.com, and donate (the max is $3,300 or $6,600 for a couple) to my campaign by Midnight EST on Dec 31st to help us beat Wiley Nickel.

Thank you so much for your time and consideration. Please do not hesitate to contact me via phone anytime: (919) 229-9310

God bless you and yours!

Respectfully,
Josh
Call Sign, "Blackjack"

Josh McConkey

[Logo, company name Description automatically generated]

PO Box 147

New Hill, NC 27562

(919) 229-9310

www.JoshMcConkey.com<https://www.joshmcconkey.com/>

https://www.facebook.com/JoshMcConkeyForCongress

Paid for by McConkey for Congress

Contributions to McConkey for Congress are not deductible as charitable donations for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, federal contractors, and foreign nationals are prohibited. All contributions are required to be made with personal funds and not those of another. Personal/Individual Credit Cards only. An individual or non-multicandidate PAC may contribute up to $6,600 ($3,300 to the primary election; $3,300 to the general election). Couples may contribute $13,200 ($6,600 to the primary election; $6,600 to the general election) from a joint account, but such a contribution requires both signatures. Multicandidate PAC’s can contribute $10,000 ($5,000 to the primary election; $5,000 to the general election).

**We can stop pretending like they’re treated equally.**

name: Kathleen Riebe
subject: Let’s continue to stand up for veterans and their families

body:

| | Happy Veterans Day, Peter. You and I both know why Veterans Day is important — it is the day we honor and celebrate those who selflessly served our country in the armed forces — but I want to share what this day means to me. The desire to serve our communities and our country runs deep in my family, and that is what led my grandfather and my great uncles to fight in World War II, and what led my cousin to serve in Vietnam. My family is no stranger to serving our country, and that is how I know service requires bravery and selflessness. But I can also tell you that service requires a lot of a veteran's family. To watch your son, daughter, spouse, or parent head off to serve your country is both inspiring and terrifying. But our families are what make service possible. The support, encouragement, and commitment families show to their soldiers and to our country reflect the spirit of our nation. So, thank you to each and every one of our veterans. Your service is powerful and vital to the United States. And thank you to the families who have supported veterans during active duty. And just as our veterans have served us, It is our responsibility to serve them. Congress must increase opportunities for veterans after their service, fully fund the VA so they can receive the health care they deserve, and protect the benefits that our veterans earned by serving our country. We cannot fully express our gratitude to our veterans. But, today, let us all shake their hands and say: "Thank you for your service." And tomorrow, let's continue to stand up and fight for our veterans and their families. Thank you, _Kathleen_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | name: Andrei Cherny
subject: Honoring the service and sacrifice of our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrei for Arizona
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73192377/437712252/1272516037?refcode=em_XX_XXXX.XX.XX_XX_XX_header_desk_of_andrei&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzZhMDc3MDktYjI4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=BKDcqKLjwI5B1bNLfPCNPaEfbbtjobExKpuSoynjf4k=) Peter –

On this Veteran’s Day, we honor the service and sacrifice of those who have answered the call to serve our great nation.

Following the 9/11 attacks, I made the decision to serve in uniform to protect the values that America represents – the freedoms and democracy that protected my family fleeing persecution, the opportunity that allowed me to build a better life. **As a U.S. Navy reserve Intelligence Officer, I witnessed firsthand the dedication and determination of my fellow service members.**

I served alongside incredible men and women who put their lives on the line. They asked for nothing in return but deserve a country that does right by them. Last year, Congress finally passed the PACT Act, which provides health benefits to veterans who were exposed to toxic substances during their service.

**The PACT Act was passed with bipartisan support – even Ted Cruz and Matt Gaetz voted for it. But David Schweikert voted no.** Seeing him vote no was one of the moments that made me decide to run for Congress.

And then, earlier this year, David Schweikert sent an email to his constituents bragging about his work for veterans and encouraging them to sign up for benefits from the PACT Act despite having voted against them. Pure hypocrisy.

We’re used to seeing Republican politicians showing up at ribbon cuttings after voting against the Infrastructure Bill. But this isn’t a road or bridge; it’s veterans’ lives. Our veterans deserve better, and that’s why I’m running for Congress.

Always in your service,

– Andrei

| To learn more about Andrei, visit [**AndreiForArizona.com**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/73192378/437712253/-651367276?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzZhMDc3MDktYjI4MC1lZTExLTg5MjUtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=BKDcqKLjwI5B1bNLfPCNPaEfbbtjobExKpuSoynjf4k=&emci=6c7c5163-b180-ee11-8925-00224832e811&emdi=36a07709-b280-ee11-8925-00224832e811&ceid=13520922) | Paid for by Andrei for Arizona

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: What Veteran’s Day Means
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
#

Friend --
Having worked alongside the U.S. military for years, Veteran’s Day is an important day for me. It’s a day for us to acknowledge the men and women, past and present, who have served our country in the armed forces.
As someone who worked alongside this great institution my whole career, and eventually served as an Assistant Secretary of Defense at the Pentagon, I want to tell you what it means to me. Most importantly, the military teaches personal leadership and putting mission before yourself. These values imbue almost everything in the military, and serve that veteran for the rest of their lives. They are the lessons that, frankly, a lot of Americans could use right now.
People take for granted that we can wake up and walk across the street, we can sit on our couch and hang out, that we can enjoy our families. We can live the life we want to live because someone is protecting us – and today is a day to show our gratitude for all who have stepped up to do just that.
I believe that service is the greatest love letter you can offer our country. Thank you today to all the active duty, Guard, Reservist veterans. You help us remember what today is all about.
Elissa Slotkin

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=790%2E135344%2Eujm4We).

Elissa Slotkin served in the Central Intelligence Agency and the Department of Defense. Use of her job titles and photographs during service do not imply endorsement by the Central Intelligence Agency OR the Department of Defense.

We are probably not the first to notice that anti-Semitic and pro-terrorist demonstrations abroad tend to be heavily young and male-dominated, but in the US, they are largely female. Perhaps this is because more women are now in college and subject to mandatory indoctrination. But that does not explain it all.

Why is almost all the “squad” female? Why do women flock to supporting a religion that would otherwise not allow them to go to school or even go out in public without male supervision? The most liberated women on earth seem to yearn for economic and social oppression.

Opposing and denigrating men in general, radical feminists are now accepting rape and sexual mutilation as an acceptable political tool. It truly is a puzzle. Perhaps the author is on to something. Organs of the culture, like movies, social media, and teen magazines, directly target young and confused females and recruit them to socialist causes. Having once accepted “democratic socialism” they now seem willing to accept socialism imposed by force and terror. Parents and grandparents need to know what their teenage daughters are reading and watching, now, more than ever.

name: Chuck Grassley
subject: Honoring our Veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Grassley Committee, Inc
body:
|
---
Dear Friends, World War I was called the "war to end all wars." The 1918 agreement to end the fighting took place on the 11th hour, of the 11th day, of the 11th month and symbolized hope for world peace and sorrow for lives lost on the battlefield. History tells us Armistice Day didn't end the war or our fight for freedom. Today, we celebrate Veterans Day on November 11. We honor those who have served our country in the U.S. Armed Forces and thank these patriots, including our military families, for their service and sacrifice. Today and every day, let us honor those who have answered the call to serve America in uniform. Today is also the 102nd anniversary of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. On Nov. 11, 1921, the remains of an unidentified soldier from World War I were buried at Arlington National Cemetery. Since then, our nation's unidentified war heroes are honored on these hallowed grounds where members of the 3rd U.S. Infantry Regiment, the Old Guard, keep watch around the clock. I'm working as hard as ever to ensure the federal government keeps its promises to our veterans and to hold the government accountable when it falls short. From protecting whistleblowers at the Veterans Administration, fixing scandalous wait times at the VA, improving suicide prevention services for veterans, protecting Second Amendment rights for veterans, and streamlining community care and access to telemedicine, I'll continue listening to the concerns and needs of Iowa veterans so they get the care and benefits they have earned. God bless our veterans. Chuck Grassley _P.S. Be sure to give a special salute to the hometown heroes in your local community. Thanks to their service and sacrifice, America is the land of the free and home of the brave. _

| Paid for by The Grassley Committee, Inc.
---

name: Gabe Vasquez
subject: Today, we honor our Veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress
body:
It's Gabe Vasquez.

Today, we honor our Veterans — all those who have stepped up to protect our nation and our freedoms.

In truth, our Veterans should be honored every day. But the reality is that many of our Veterans find themselves left behind by our country.

As a member of Congress, I’m committed to ensuring New Mexico’s Veterans have access to the best programs and care available. I will always work to ensure that those who served and sacrificed for our country receive the care and support they deserve.

I want to extend my sincerest thanks to all who have served, and I hope you will join me in reflecting upon the sacrifices that so many have made to defend our democracy.

Gracias,
Gabe

name: Angela
subject: Our vets deserve action.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2571?t=1006&akid=650%2E52803%2Eh8RZwd ][IMG]

Happy Veteran’s Day, Peter!

Today is a special day, when we get to honor and celebrate the brave
servicemen and women – folks who’ve made immense personal sacrifices for
the greater good of our nation.

The truth is, words alone can’t repay the debt of gratitude we owe our
veterans. We’ve got to repay it with action.

Prince George’s County is home to 60,000 veterans, far too many of whom
can’t access the benefits and resources promised to them: quality mental
and physical healthcare, affordable housing, decent job opportunities, and
support for those who can’t work.

So, I took action. My administration created Maryland’s first county-level
Office of Veterans Affairs to improve quality of life for all veterans
here in Prince George’s.

And as Senator, I’ll continue to be a fierce advocate for those who have
served – to ensure all veterans can live in safety and peace.

Happy Veteran’s Day!

–Angela Alsobrooks
